So the thing is, is you start where you are and then you just get better from there. Now it is much easier to do full motion video with a teleprompter and basically a teleprompter. It's a device where you are looking at the camera. Well it's something that fits in front of your camera and it will have your script on the camera. So you're looking at your script but you're actually the camera is right behind the script and you can read the script while you're looking straight into the camera and they're pretty darn cool. I've got one and I use one and I like it. I'll get a little more into that a little later on. If you want to shoot outside, teleprompters really don't work very well outside. First of all, you need a long extension cord depending on where you're at but then it's usually just too bright to be able to really read the teleprompters. There is some that work outside but they tend to be a lot more expensive. Just for the most part teleprompters don't work outside all that well. ## [06:01 - 09:01]

Especially if you can hook up an external audio for your, for your phone and like you know on my iPhone I can do that. I can, I got like a little add on that allows me to run a separate mic and then you know, so we have external audio. So external audio input is very important. Whatever camera you get, again it's not, you don't absolutely need it because I did do really well with that hundred dollar camera with on camera mic but when you start shopping for a camera I would look for an external audio input because you, just like with screen cap you want to have good audio and if you can run a mic into your camera then you know that's really great. Now you can always use like a little digital audio recorder that's separate from your camera if your camera, if your camera does not have an external audio input you can always use record audio with a digital audio recorder, an external device and then in your editing match up the audio in the videos. It's a little more complicated but you know I've got a couple platinum members that really do that very, very well. And then another thing that's really nice to have and this tends to be in more advanced cameras is audio monitoring so that those who are shooting your video can actually plug in a headset and make sure that the audio you're capturing is good audio. This is, I can't tell you how many times we've lost shots because we thought we had good audio but then we got back because we didn't have that audio monitoring and then we got back into the studio and found out we did not have good audio. This happens a lot like if you're shooting outside there might be a little wind and you're trying to decide if you're picking up wind noise.
